title: AWS Course
description: End-to-end AWS training from fundamentals to advanced architecture, DevOps, security, networking, data, and serverless—balanced with hands-on labs and capstone projects.
prerequisites: Basic knowledge of networking and IT concepts

courseStructure.title: Cloud Fundamentals & AWS Account Setup
topics: AWS global infrastructure: Regions, AZs, Edge
AWS Free Tier, account setup, billing basics

courseStructure.title: Networking Deep Dive
topics: VPC, subnets, route tables, IGW, NAT
Security groups vs NACLs, VPC endpoints (Gateway/Interface)
Load Balancers: ALB, NLB, Gateway LB

courseStructure.title: Compute & Containers
topics: EC2 families, AMIs, EBS/Ephemeral, Placement groups
Pricing models: On-Demand, RI, Savings Plans, Spot
Containers: ECR, ECS, Fargate, EKS (clusters, nodes, add-ons)

courseStructure.title: Storage & Content Delivery
topics: S3 buckets, prefixes, object lifecycle, versioning
Storage classes: Standard, IA, One Zone-IA, Glacier tiers
S3 security: bucket policies, ACLs, SSE-KMS, S3 Block Public Access

courseStructure.title: Databases & Caching
topics: RDS engines (MySQL, PostgreSQL, MariaDB, SQL Server, Oracle)
DynamoDB (PK/SK, GSIs/LSIs, capacity modes, DAX, TTL)

courseStructure.title: Serverless & Integration
topics: EventBridge vs SNS vs SQS patterns

courseStructure.title: Data Engineering & Analytics (Intro)
topics: Glue (crawlers, ETL jobs), Glue Data Catalog

courseStructure.title: Capstone Projects & Labs
topics: Project 1: 3-tier web app (ALB + ASG + RDS) with IaC & CI/CD
Project 2: Serverless API (API GW + Lambda + DynamoDB + Cognito + S3)
Project 3: Data lake mini-pipeline (S3 + Glue + Athena + QuickSight)
Bonus Labs: EKS on Fargate or ECS blue/green with CodeDeploy

courseStructure.title: Certification Path (Optional)
topics: Exam mapping: Cloud Practitioner → SAA-C03 → DVA-C02/DBS-C01/SOA-C02 (overview)
Hands-on focus areas per exam blueprint

title: Microsoft Azure Course
description: Learn Microsoft Azure from scratch — covering cloud fundamentals, core Azure services, security, networking, DevOps tools, and hands-on deployments.
prerequisites: Basic knowledge of IT concepts, networking, and operating systems

courseStructure.title: Introduction to Cloud and Azure
topics: Azure Global Infrastructure (Regions, Availability Zones, Edge Locations)
Azure Portal and Azure Resource Explorer

courseStructure.title: Storage Services
topics: Blob, File, Queue, and Table Storage

courseStructure.title: Networking in Azure
topics: Subnets, IPs, and Network Security Groups (NSG)

courseStructure.title: Monitoring and Security
topics: Microsoft Defender for Cloud (formerly Security Center)

courseStructure.title: DevOps and Automation (Intro)
topics: Infrastructure as Code with ARM Templates and Bicep

courseStructure.title: Project and Deployment
topics: Mini Project: Deploy a Web App with Database
Hosting a Static Website on Azure Storage
VM Deployment with Networking and Security

title: Google Cloud Platform (GCP)
description: Comprehensive GCP training covering cloud fundamentals, networking, compute, storage, databases, security, DevOps, AI/ML services, and hands-on labs aligned with industry use cases and certification paths.
prerequisites: Basic knowledge of networking and IT concepts

courseStructure.title: Cloud Fundamentals & GCP Account Setup
topics: Google Cloud global infrastructure: Regions, Zones, Edge locations
GCP resource hierarchy: Organizations, Folders, Projects

courseStructure.title: Compute & Containers
topics: Compute Engine: VM families, images, disks
Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE): clusters, node pools, add-ons

courseStructure.title: Storage & Content Delivery
topics: Cloud Storage buckets: classes (Standard, Nearline, Coldline, Archive)

courseStructure.title: Databases & Data Warehousing
topics: BigQuery (data warehouse, DML, partitions, federated queries)

courseStructure.title: Observability & Operations
topics: Cloud Logging & Cloud Monitoring (metrics, dashboards, alerts)

courseStructure.title: Security & Compliance
topics: Cloud Armor (DDoS protection), Web Security Scanner

courseStructure.title: DevOps & IaC on GCP
topics: Blue/Green & Canary deployments with GKE/Cloud Run

courseStructure.title: AI & Machine Learning on GCP
topics: Vertex AI: pipelines, AutoML, model training/deployment
Pre-trained APIs: Vision, Speech, Translation, NLP

courseStructure.title: Capstone Projects & Labs
topics: Project 1: Deploy 3-tier web app on Compute Engine + Cloud SQL + Load Balancer
Project 2: Serverless API with Cloud Functions + API Gateway + Firestore
Project 3: Data analytics pipeline (Pub/Sub + Dataflow + BigQuery + Looker Studio)
Bonus Labs: GKE with CI/CD on Cloud Build

courseStructure.title: Certification Path (Optional)
topics: Exam mapping: Associate Cloud Engineer → Professional Cloud Architect / Data Engineer
Hands-on focus areas per exam blueprint

title: Docker Beginner Course
description: Learn Docker from scratch to containerize applications, manage images, and deploy lightweight, portable, and scalable solutions.
prerequisites: Basic knowledge of Linux and command line

courseStructure.title: Security and Optimization
topics: Secrets Management (Compose secrets, env best practices)

courseStructure.title: Orchestration Basics
topics: When to choose Compose vs Swarm vs Kubernetes

title: Kubernetes Course
description: Learn Kubernetes to orchestrate, scale, and manage containerized applications efficiently in production environments.
prerequisites: Basic knowledge of Docker and Linux

courseStructure.title: Introduction to Kubernetes
topics: Control Plane & Node Components (API Server, Scheduler, Kubelet)
Setting up a Local Cluster (Minikube / kind)

courseStructure.title: Security in Kubernetes
topics: Encrypting Secrets at rest (KMS plugin intro)

courseStructure.title: Cloud & Production
topics: Cost basics: requests/limits, right-sizing, bin packing

courseStructure.title: Project and Deployment
topics: Mini Project: Multi-tier app on Kubernetes (API + DB + Frontend)

title: Linux Course
description: Learn Linux fundamentals, essential commands, shell scripting, and system navigation to become confident in using Linux for development and administration.
prerequisites: None (basic computer knowledge recommended)

courseStructure.title: Introduction to Linux
topics: Navigating the Shell (TTY vs Terminal, bash/zsh)

courseStructure.title: Project and Practice
topics: Mini Project: Automated Backup Script (tar + cron)
System Health Check Script (disk, memory, services)

prerequisites: Basic knowledge of programming and command line

courseStructure.title: Working with Repositories

courseStructure.title: Project and Best Practices
topics: Mini Project: Collaborative Repository with PRs

prerequisites: Basic knowledge of HTML, CSS, and JavaScript

courseStructure.title: Project and Testing
topics: Mini Project: Todo App / Weather App
Introduction to Unit Testing with Jest

title: Angular Course
description: Learn Angular step by step to build dynamic, component-driven, single-page applications with TypeScript.
prerequisites: Basic knowledge of HTML, CSS, and JavaScript

title: Next.js Course
description: Learn Next.js from scratch to build modern, server-rendered, and optimized full-stack web applications with React.
prerequisites: Basic knowledge of React.js, HTML, CSS, and JavaScript

courseStructure.title: API Routes
topics: Handling GET, POST, PUT, DELETE Requests

courseStructure.title: Image and Asset Optimization
topics: SEO Basics with next/head and Metadata API

courseStructure.title: Deployment
topics: Deploying to Other Platforms (Netlify, AWS, Azure)

courseStructure.title: Project and Testing
topics: Mini Project: Blog App with Authentication

title: TypeScript Course
description: Learn TypeScript from scratch and write safer, cleaner, and more scalable JavaScript code for modern web applications.
prerequisites: Good understanding of JavaScript fundamentals (variables, functions, objects, arrays, ES6 basics)

courseStructure.title: Introduction to TypeScript
topics: What is TypeScript and why use it?
Setting up a basic TypeScript project

courseStructure.title: Interfaces and Type Aliases
topics: Using interfaces with functions and objects

courseStructure.title: Generics (Beginner Level)
topics: Working with generics in arrays and utility functions

courseStructure.title: Modules and Project Structure
topics: Organizing code into files and folders

courseStructure.title: TypeScript with React or Node.js (Overview)
topics: Using TypeScript in a React project (props and state typing – overview)

courseStructure.title: Mini Project and Best Practices
topics: Mini Project: Typed Todo App or API Client
Avoiding any and improving type safety
Next steps: Advanced types, generics, and frameworks

title: Data Structures Course
description: Learn core data structures step-by-step and understand how arrays, linked lists, stacks, queues, trees, graphs, and hash maps actually work under the hood. Build a strong foundation for problem-solving, competitive coding, and technical interviews.
prerequisites: Basic programming knowledge in at least one language (Java, Python, C, or JavaScript) — variables, loops, functions, and arrays.

courseStructure.title: Introduction to Data Structures
topics: What are data structures and why they matter
Types of data structures (linear vs non-linear)
How data structures relate to real-world problems

courseStructure.title: Linked Lists
topics: Singly linked list – concept and implementation
Insertion and deletion in linked lists
Doubly linked lists – when and why
Common linked list interview patterns (reverse, middle, cycle)

courseStructure.title: Stacks and Queues
topics: Stack using array and linked list
Priority queue (intro) and real-world use cases

courseStructure.title: Searching and Sorting Basics
topics: Selection sort, bubble sort, insertion sort (concepts)
Where sorting and searching appear in interviews

courseStructure.title: Hashing and Hash Maps
topics: Use cases: frequency counting, lookups, caching
Common hash map based interview problems

courseStructure.title: Trees and Binary Search Trees
topics: Tree terminology (node, root, leaf, height, depth)
Binary trees vs binary search trees (BST)
Tree traversals (inorder, preorder, postorder, level order)
Basic BST operations (insert, search, min, max)

courseStructure.title: Heaps and Priority Queues
topics: Use cases: top K elements, scheduling, Dijkstra (intro)

courseStructure.title: Graphs (Conceptual Introduction)
topics: Graph basics – vertices, edges, adjacency list, adjacency matrix
BFS and DFS (concept and simple use cases)
Real world examples: social networks, maps, dependencies

courseStructure.title: Problem-Solving Practice & Mini Project
topics: Step-by-step approach to solving DSA problems
Practice set: arrays, strings, stacks/queues, hash maps
Mini project: simple data-structure-powered feature (like undo history, playlist manager, or task scheduler)
Next steps: advanced algorithms, competitive programming, and interview prep

title: Flask Course
description: Complete Flask training that covers Python web fundamentals, routing, templates, databases, authentication, APIs, testing, deployment, and advanced extensions—balanced with hands-on projects and real-world use cases.
prerequisites: Basic knowledge of Python programming

courseStructure.title: Flask Routing & Views
topics: Handling HTTP methods (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE)

courseStructure.title: Capstone Projects & Labs
topics: Project 1: Blog platform with authentication & database
Project 2: REST API with JWT authentication

courseStructure.title: Certification & Next Steps

title: MySQL Course
description: Learn SQL using MySQL — one of the most popular open-source relational database systems. Covers SQL fundamentals, MySQL tools, and practical hands-on projects.

courseStructure.title: Introduction to MySQL

courseStructure.title: MySQL DQL (Data Query Language)

Joins in MySQL (INNER, LEFT, RIGHT, CROSS)

courseStructure.title: MySQL DCL & TCL

courseStructure.title: Constraints and Keys

courseStructure.title: Project and Practice

title: PostgreSQL Course
description: Learn SQL using PostgreSQL — a powerful open-source relational database with advanced features like JSON support, window functions, and CTEs.
prerequisites: Basic computer knowledge; no prior SQL experience required

courseStructure.title: PostgreSQL DDL (Data Definition Language)

courseStructure.title: PostgreSQL DQL (Data Query Language)

title: Python Language Course

courseStructure.title: Python Strings

title: Snowflake Cloud Engineering
description: Learn Snowflake end-to-end for modern cloud data engineering: loading data, modeling, performance tuning, security, automation with Streams/Tasks, and Python integration using Snowpark. Build an enterprise-style ELT pipeline and a portfolio-ready capstone project.
prerequisites: Comfortable with basic SQL (SELECT, JOIN, GROUP BY). Basic Python is helpful but not mandatory. Cloud fundamentals are a plus.

courseStructure.title: Introduction to Snowflake & Cloud Data Warehousing
topics: Why Snowflake? Cloud Data Warehouse vs Traditional DW
Snowflake Architecture: Storage, Compute, Cloud Services
Key Concepts: Database, Schema, Warehouse, Role

courseStructure.title: Core SQL in Snowflake
topics: Joins, Window Functions, CTEs (Quick refresh with Snowflake)
Views vs Materialized Views (When to use)

courseStructure.title: Data Loading & File Handling
topics: Bulk Load Patterns & File Naming conventions
Validating Loads: Row counts, rejects, data quality checks

courseStructure.title: Warehouses, Performance & Cost Optimization
topics: Query profile: reading execution plans and bottlenecks
Performance techniques: pruning, selective filters, join strategies
Cost controls: warehouse management and usage tracking (intro)

courseStructure.title: Data Modeling in Snowflake (Dimensional)
topics: Surrogate keys, SCD Type 1/2 patterns (high level + implementation)
Model validation: referential checks and reconciliation

courseStructure.title: Security, Governance & Access Control
topics: Auditing basics: access history & query history
Data sharing overview (secure data share concepts)

courseStructure.title: Automation with Streams, Tasks & Snowpipe
topics: Operational reliability: retries, error tables, monitoring patterns

courseStructure.title: Python Integration with Snowpark
topics: Snowpark overview: when to use vs SQL

courseStructure.title: Production Readiness: Monitoring & Operations
topics: Monitoring warehouse usage and query performance
Time Travel, Cloning, and recovery strategies
Release checklist: testing, validation, rollback approach

courseStructure.title: Capstone Project (Portfolio Ready)
topics: Capstone: Build an end-to-end ELT pipeline in Snowflake
Final deliverables: GitHub README, architecture diagram, and demo walkthrough

title: HTML Fundamentals for Web Development
description: Learn the fundamentals of HTML and how it forms the backbone of every web page. Master HTML tags, forms, semantic elements, and web accessibility for building modern, responsive websites.
prerequisites: No prior knowledge required. Basic understanding of computers and the internet is helpful.

courseStructure.title: Introduction to HTML
topics: What is HTML? Understanding the structure of HTML documents
HTML Tags: Elements, Attributes, and Nesting
Basic HTML Document Structure: DOCTYPE, html, head, and body tags
The Role of HTML in Web Development

courseStructure.title: HTML Text Formatting and Structure
topics:
Line Breaks: `
` and White Space Handling

courseStructure.title: Working with Images and Multimedia
topics: Embedding Audio and Video with `<audio>` and `<video>`
Using `<iframe>` for Embedding External Content (YouTube, Maps, etc.)

courseStructure.title: HTML Forms and Input Handling

courseStructure.title: HTML Tables and Data Presentation

courseStructure.title: Semantic HTML and Accessibility

courseStructure.title: HTML5 New Elements and Features

courseStructure.title: Responsive Web Design with HTML

courseStructure.title: Advanced HTML Techniques
topics: HTML Forms: Advanced Use Cases (Multi-step forms, File Uploads)

Implementing Browser Features with HTML5 APIs (Geolocation, Web Storage)

title: CSS Fundamentals for Web Development
description: Learn the fundamentals of CSS to style and design modern, responsive websites. From basic styles to advanced layout techniques, this course covers everything you need to master CSS for web development.
prerequisites: Basic understanding of HTML. No prior CSS experience is required.

courseStructure.title: Introduction to CSS

topics: Basic CSS Selectors: Element, Class, ID selectors

courseStructure.title: Text and Font Styling

courseStructure.title: Responsive Design and Media Queries

courseStructure.title: CSS Transitions and Animations

courseStructure.title: Advanced CSS Techniques
topics: Custom Properties (CSS Variables): Defining and Using Variables

CSS Frameworks Overview: Bootstrap, Tailwind CSS, and Material Design

title: Bootstrap for Responsive Web Design
description: Learn how to create responsive, mobile-first websites using Bootstrap, a popular front-end framework. Master its grid system, components, utilities, and themes to build modern, visually appealing websites.
prerequisites: Basic knowledge of HTML and CSS is required. No prior experience with Bootstrap needed.

courseStructure.title: Introduction to Bootstrap
topics: What is Bootstrap? Overview of the framework
Setting up Bootstrap: CDN vs. Local installation
Bootstrap Folder Structure: CSS, JS, and Fonts
Bootstrap Grid System: Introduction and Concept

courseStructure.title: Bootstrap Grid System
topics: Aligning and Justifying Content in Grids

courseStructure.title: Bootstrap Typography
topics: Font Styles: Default and Customizing Fonts

courseStructure.title: Bootstrap Forms
topics: Input Types: Text, Password, Radio, Checkbox, Date, etc.
Form Validation and Feedback with `.is-valid` and `.is-invalid`
Customizing Form Controls: Selects, Inputs, and Checkboxes

courseStructure.title: Bootstrap Buttons and Links
topics: Button Variants: Primary, Secondary, Success, etc.
Links and Link Buttons: Styling links as buttons

courseStructure.title: Bootstrap Components
topics: Modals: Creating and Customizing Modal Windows

courseStructure.title: Bootstrap Utilities
topics: Spacing Utilities: `.m-*`, `.p-*` for margin and padding
Text Utilities: `.text-*` for color, alignment, and transformation

courseStructure.title: Responsive Design with Bootstrap
topics: Creating Responsive Layouts with the Grid System
Visibility and Display Utilities for Different Devices

courseStructure.title: Customizing Bootstrap Themes
topics: Overriding Default Styles with Custom CSS

courseStructure.title: Capstone Project (Portfolio Ready)
topics: Capstone: Build a Portfolio Website using Bootstrap
Create a Landing Page with Navigation, Cards, and Forms
Implement a Fully Responsive Layout for Multiple Devices
Integrate Bootstrap Components like Modals and Alerts
Final Deliverables: GitHub repository with code, README, and demo walkthrough

title: SQL Server for Database Development and Administration
description: Learn SQL Server from both a development and administration perspective. This course covers SQL queries, database design, performance tuning, security, and backup strategies, ensuring you can manage and optimize SQL Server databases effectively.
prerequisites: Basic understanding of databases and SQL queries. Familiarity with relational database concepts is recommended.

courseStructure.title: Introduction to SQL Server
topics: What is SQL Server? Overview of Microsoft SQL Server
SQL Server Editions: Choosing the Right Edition
Installing SQL Server: Steps and Configuration
SQL Server Databases: Understanding Database Architecture

courseStructure.title: SQL Server Basic Querying
topics: Basic SQL Syntax: SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE
Filtering Data: WHERE Clause, Comparison Operators
Using Aggregates: COUNT, SUM, AVG, MIN, MAX
Joining Tables: INNER JOIN, LEFT JOIN, RIGHT JOIN

courseStructure.title: Advanced SQL Queries
topics: Window Functions: ROW_NUMBER(), RANK(), LEAD(), LAG()
Working with NULLs: IS NULL, IS NOT NULL
String Functions: CONCAT, SUBSTRING, CHARINDEX, REPLACE

courseStructure.title: SQL Server Data Types and Constraints
topics: Understanding Data Types: INT, VARCHAR, DATETIME, etc.
Working with Numbers: DECIMAL, NUMERIC, FLOAT
Date and Time Functions: GETDATE(), DATEDIFF()
Implementing Constraints: PRIMARY KEY, FOREIGN KEY, UNIQUE

courseStructure.title: SQL Server Views, Indexes, and Stored Procedures
topics: Creating and Using Views: Benefits and Use Cases
Stored Procedures: Creating, Altering, and Executing

courseStructure.title: SQL Server Database Design and Normalization
SQL Server Security
topics: SQL Server Authentication: Windows vs SQL Server Authentication
Roles and Permissions: GRANT, DENY, REVOKE
Encryption: Transparent Data Encryption (TDE), Always Encrypted
SQL Injection Prevention and Best Practices

courseStructure.title: SQL Server Backup and Recovery
topics: Backup Strategies: Full, Differential, Transaction Log
Restoring Databases: Full Restore, Point-in-Time Restore
Automating Backups with SQL Server Agent
Database Mirroring and Always On Availability Groups
High Availability and Disaster Recovery Strategies

courseStructure.title: SQL Server Advanced Topics
topics: Working with XML and JSON in SQL Server

courseStructure.title: Capstone Project (Portfolio Ready)
topics: Capstone: Design and Implement a Full SQL Server Database Solution
Create a Database Schema with Tables, Relationships, and Constraints
Write Complex SQL Queries for Data Retrieval and Manipulation
Implement Stored Procedures, Views, and Triggers for Business Logic
Optimize Database Performance and Implement Security Best Practices
Final Deliverables: GitHub repository with code, README, and demo walkthrough

title: AWS Cloud Foundation to Professional Data Engineering
description: This program takes you from AWS cloud fundamentals to professional-level data engineering and production architecture. It covers core AWS services, security, networking, compute, storage, and then deep-dives into modern data lake, analytics, governance, DevOps, and enterprise-scale patterns used in real-world data platforms.
prerequisites: Basic IT knowledge is helpful but not required. Familiarity with databases, SQL, or programming will be an advantage.

courseStructure.title: Foundations & Account Setup
topics: Regions, Availability Zones, Edge Locations, Endpoints, Shared Responsibility Model
AWS Account Setup: MFA, Budgets, Billing Alarms, Cost Allocation Tags
AWS CLI and SDK, Named Profiles

courseStructure.title: Identity & Access Management (IAM, Identity Center, Organizations)
topics: IAM Users, Roles, Policies, Least Privilege, Permission Boundaries
IAM Access Analyzer, Credential Report, IAM Best Practices
Identity Center: Users, Groups, Permission Sets, SSO Apps, SCIM Basics
AWS Organizations: Multi-Account Strategy, OUs, SCPs

courseStructure.title: Networking (VPC)
topics: CIDR Planning, Public and Private Subnets, Route Tables
Internet Gateway, NAT Gateway vs NAT Instance
Hybrid Connectivity: Site-to-Site VPN, Direct Connect

courseStructure.title: Compute (EC2, Auto Scaling, ELB)
topics: EC2 Instance Families, AMIs, User Data, Instance Profiles
On-Demand vs Spot vs Savings Plans Basics
Elastic Load Balancer: ALB vs NLB vs GWLB, Target Groups, Health Checks
Auto Scaling Policies, Lifecycle Hooks, Warm Pools

courseStructure.title: Storage (Core AWS Storage)
topics: S3 Buckets and Prefixes, Versioning, Lifecycle Policies
Replication and Encryption: SSE-S3, SSE-KMS, Client-Side
S3 Access Points, VPC-Only Access, Presigned URLs
EFS and FSx (Windows, Lustre, ONTAP, OpenZFS)

courseStructure.title: Databases (Core)
topics: RDS Engines, Multi-AZ, Read Replicas, Backups, Performance Insights
Aurora Provisioned and Serverless v2, Replicas, Global Concepts
DynamoDB: PK/SK Design, GSIs, LSIs, Streams, TTL, DAX, Capacity Modes

courseStructure.title: App Integration (Messaging & Eventing)
topics: SNS and SQS (Standard and FIFO), DLQs, Retries, Idempotency
Amazon MQ Basics and Use Cases

courseStructure.title: Serverless
topics: AWS Lambda: Concurrency, Layers, Provisioned vs On-Demand
API Gateway: REST, HTTP, WebSocket APIs

courseStructure.title: Containers
topics: ECS with EC2 and Fargate, Service Discovery, Autoscaling

courseStructure.title: Observability & Operations
topics: CloudWatch Metrics, Logs, Alarms, Log Insights
Systems Manager: Session Manager, Patch Manager, Inventory, Automation Runbooks

courseStructure.title: Data Engineering Fundamentals
topics: ETL vs ELT, Incremental Loads, Watermarking
Data SLAs, SLOs, Backfills, Reprocessing Strategies

courseStructure.title: Data Lake & Lakehouse Architecture
topics: S3 Lake Layout: Bronze, Silver, Gold

courseStructure.title: Ingestion & Data Movement
topics: AWS DMS Full Load and CDC
Firehose Delivery to S3, Redshift, OpenSearch

courseStructure.title: Catalog, Governance & Access Control
topics: Lake Formation Permissions, LF-Tags, Row/Column Security

courseStructure.title: Transformation & Processing Engines
topics: Glue ETL Jobs, Crawlers, Parameters, Retries
Glue Studio vs Script, DynamicFrames vs DataFrames

courseStructure.title: Query & Analytics Layer
topics: Redshift RA3 and Serverless, Dist and Sort Keys

courseStructure.title: Data Quality & Testing
topics: Completeness, Uniqueness, Referential, Range, Freshness Rules
Unit Tests and Data Tests for Pipelines

courseStructure.title: Performance & Cost Optimization (FinOps)
topics: S3 Lifecycle and Storage Class Optimization
Cost & Usage Reports with Athena and QuickSight

courseStructure.title: Backup, DR & Resiliency
topics: DR Patterns: Pilot Light, Warm Standby, Active-Active

courseStructure.title: Capstone Project (Portfolio Ready)
topics: Design and Implement an End-to-End AWS Data Platform
Transform and Model Data for Analytics
Implement Governance, Security, and Cost Controls
Final Deliverables: GitHub Repo, Architecture Diagram, Demo Walkthrough

title: MongoDB for Database Development
description: Learn how to work with MongoDB, a popular NoSQL database, and build applications that store data in a flexible and scalable way. This course covers everything from basic CRUD operations to advanced topics like indexing, aggregation, and data modeling.
prerequisites: Basic knowledge of databases and SQL. Familiarity with programming concepts is helpful but not required.

courseStructure.title: Introduction to MongoDB
topics: What is MongoDB? Overview of NoSQL and MongoDB
Installing MongoDB: Local and Cloud (MongoDB Atlas)
MongoDB vs Relational Databases: Pros and Cons

courseStructure.title: MongoDB CRUD Operations

courseStructure.title: MongoDB Data Modeling
topics: Document Structure and Design: BSON and Data Types
Best Practices for Modeling Documents and Collections
Schema Design for Read and Write Efficiency
Handling Arrays, Subdocuments, and Nested Data

courseStructure.title: Indexes in MongoDB
topics: What are Indexes? Why Use Them?
Types of Indexes: Single-field, Compound, Geospatial, Text Indexes
Managing Indexes: Listing, Dropping, and Rebuilding Indexes
Understanding Indexing Performance and Best Practices

courseStructure.title: Aggregation in MongoDB
topics: Using `$lookup` for Joins and `$unwind` for Arrays

courseStructure.title: MongoDB Transactions
topics: Best Practices for Transactional Data in MongoDB

courseStructure.title: Replication and High Availability
topics: Ensuring High Availability and Data Redundancy

courseStructure.title: Sharding in MongoDB
topics: Sharding Key: Choosing the Right Key
Sharded Clusters: Config Servers, Query Routers, Shard Servers

courseStructure.title: MongoDB Security
topics: Auditing MongoDB: Audit Logs and Compliance

courseStructure.title: MongoDB Performance Optimization
topics: Optimizing Queries: Query Performance and Indexing

courseStructure.title: Capstone Project (Portfolio Ready)
topics: Capstone: Build a Full-Stack Application using MongoDB
Create a Data Model for a Real-World Application
Implement MongoDB CRUD Operations, Indexing, and Aggregation
Design and Implement a Scalable, High-Availability MongoDB Solution
Final Deliverables: GitHub repository with code, README, and demo walkthrough

title: Git for Version Control and Collaboration
description: Learn how to use Git for version control, managing repositories, collaborating with teams, and automating development workflows. This course covers basic and advanced Git concepts for developers.
prerequisites: Basic understanding of software development and programming concepts.

courseStructure.title: Introduction to Git and Version Control
topics: What is Git? Introduction to Version Control Systems
Setting Up Git: Installation and Configuration

Basic Git Commands: `git init`, `git status`, `git commit`, `git push`, `git pull`
Branching and Merging: Creating, Merging, and Deleting Branches
Collaborating with Git: Forking, Pull Requests, and Code Reviews
Git Best Practices: Commit Messages, Branching Strategies (GitFlow, GitHub Flow)

title: GitHub Actions for CI/CD Automation
description: Learn to automate build, test, and deployment workflows using GitHub Actions. This course covers workflow creation, CI/CD automation, and advanced GitHub Actions configurations.
prerequisites: Basic knowledge of Git and GitHub.

courseStructure.title: GitHub Actions Overview

Best Practices for Organizing GitHub Actions Workflows

title: GitLab CI/CD for Continuous Integration
description: Master GitLab CI/CD pipelines and automate your development workflows using GitLab. Learn to configure pipelines, manage runners, and optimize your deployments.
prerequisites: Familiarity with Git and GitLab repositories.

title: CircleCI for Continuous Integration and Delivery
description: Learn CircleCI to automate the development lifecycle from testing to deployment. This course covers CircleCI pipeline configuration, parallelism, and optimization techniques.
prerequisites: Familiarity with Git and basic CI/CD concepts.

courseStructure.title: CircleCI Fundamentals

title: Azure DevOps for CI/CD Automation
description: This course covers Azure DevOps services for version control, build pipelines, release management, and deployment. Learn to automate CI/CD with Azure Pipelines and integrate with other Microsoft Azure services.
prerequisites: Familiarity with version control and basic DevOps practices.

description: Learn Jenkins for automating the build, test, and deployment pipelines in software development. This course covers Jenkins installation, creating pipelines, and using Jenkins for continuous integration.
prerequisites: Basic knowledge of version control systems and software development.

title: AWS Amplify for Full-Stack Web and Mobile Development

prerequisites: Basic understanding of Git and version control systems.

courseStructure.title: Introduction to Bitbucket
topics: What is Bitbucket? Overview of Version Control with Bitbucket

title: Snowpark for Data Engineering & Advanced Analytics
description: This course focuses on Snowpark, Snowflake’s developer framework, enabling data engineers and developers to build scalable data transformations, machine learning pipelines, and advanced analytics directly inside Snowflake using Python, Java, and Scala. You will learn how to replace external Spark jobs with Snowpark-native workloads for better performance, governance, and cost efficiency.
prerequisites: Strong SQL knowledge, basic Python programming, and familiarity with Snowflake concepts such as warehouses, stages, and security roles.

courseStructure.title: Introduction to Snowpark & Developer Architecture
topics: What is Snowpark and Why It Matters
Snowpark vs Spark vs SQL-Based ELT

courseStructure.title: Snowpark Setup & Development Environment
topics: Roles, Warehouses, and Permissions for Developers

courseStructure.title: Snowpark DataFrames & Transformations
topics: Creating DataFrames from Tables, Views, and Stages
Selecting, Filtering, Sorting, and Aggregating Data

courseStructure.title: Working with Semi-Structured Data in Snowpark

topics: Build a Production-Grade Snowpark Data Pipeline
Implement Incremental ELT using Streams & Tasks
Final Deliverables: GitHub Repo, Architecture Diagram, Demo Walkthrough

title: Snowpipe for Continuous Data Ingestion in Snowflake
description: This course focuses on Snowpipe, Snowflake’s continuous data ingestion service. You will learn how to design, implement, monitor, and optimize real-time and near-real-time ingestion pipelines from cloud storage and streaming sources into Snowflake using Snowpipe, Snowpipe Streaming, and event-driven architectures.
prerequisites: Basic Snowflake knowledge, SQL fundamentals, and familiarity with cloud storage (S3, Azure Blob, or GCS).

courseStructure.title: Introduction to Snowpipe
topics: What is Snowpipe and When to Use It

courseStructure.title: Security, Cost & Governance

courseStructure.title: Snowpipe Integration Patterns
topics: Snowpipe with AWS S3, Azure Blob, GCS

courseStructure.title: Capstone Project (Portfolio Ready)
topics: Design a Real-Time Ingestion Pipeline with Snowpipe
Final Deliverables: GitHub Repo, Architecture Diagram, Demo Walkthrough

title: Terraform Infrastructure as Code (IaC) for Cloud & Data Platforms
description: This course provides hands-on training on Terraform for building, managing, and automating cloud infrastructure using Infrastructure as Code (IaC). You will learn how to provision AWS, Azure, GCP, and data platform resources using reusable, version-controlled Terraform modules following production best practices.
prerequisites: Basic cloud knowledge (AWS/Azure/GCP) and familiarity with Linux or command-line tools is recommended.

title: Apache Kafka for Real-Time Data Streaming & Event-Driven Systems
description: This course provides end-to-end training on Apache Kafka, covering core concepts, producer-consumer patterns, stream processing, schema management, security, and operations. You will learn how to build scalable, fault-tolerant, real-time data pipelines and event-driven architectures using Kafka in production environments.

title: Apache Airflow for Workflow Orchestration & Data Pipelines
description: This course provides hands-on training on Apache Airflow for designing, scheduling, and monitoring data pipelines and workflows. You will learn how to build reliable, scalable, and production-grade orchestration systems for batch, streaming, ML, and analytics workloads using Airflow in real-world environments.
prerequisites: Python fundamentals, basic SQL, and understanding of data pipelines or cloud platforms is recommended.
